title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
edition_pinned: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild (Nintendo Switch / Wii U, March 2017 launch) — chemistry+physics open-world rules as shipped; not Tears of the Kingdom.
moat: Shared chemistry+physics state rules link almost every object and element, enabling unique player-authored solutions to problems and puzzles (including combat uses) rather than single scripted paths.

loop_skeleton.description: Survey terrain, spend stamina on climb or glider paths, apply weapons and elemental states to enemies or materials, cook consumables for the next push, clear a shrine or tower objective that refunds map options into the open field.

depth_mechanisms.name: chemistry engine
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: A rule-based state calculator tracks element states (fire, ice, electricity, wind, water) so elements change material states and each other; materials do not change each other. The same rules drive puzzles, combat, and traversal.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If chemistry states are removed and only rigid scripted puzzle flags remain, players stop inventing off-script combat and route solutions after session one.

depth_mechanisms.name: weapon durability budget
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Weapons break after a finite number of hits, so inventory slots become temporary tools rather than permanent loadouts; the player must choose when to spend a high-damage weapon and where to restock from the field.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If durability is removed and strongest weapons never break, players stop picking up field weapons and combat converges on a fixed favorite loadout by session two.

depth_mechanisms.name: stamina-gated vertical traversal
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Climbing, gliding, swimming, and running draw from one stamina pool. Most surfaces are climbable until stamina empties; cooking and gear can fund stamina and resistance buffs (heat/cold), so route choice is a stamina and preparation decision rather than a pathfinder unlock.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If climb and glider cost no stamina, second-session route planning that budgets rest, food, and environmental preparation stops mattering for major landmarks.

feel_signature: A climb or glider path spends stamina; weapons and shields degrade through combat use and can shatter; success is a player-composed route or element interaction, not a scripted set-piece completion.
second_session_answer: Weapon breakage plus field restock pressure incentivize ongoing exploration for replacements; reusable chemistry interactions (metal+lightning, fire+grass) remain the problem-solving vocabulary rather than a skill-tree grind. The card does not claim observed second-session return routing toward towers/shrines.
packaging_lessons: Inference: ship a small closed test bed of chemistry and physics rules (Great Plateau style) before full open-world scale so rule consistency can be proven where failure is cheap.

Inference: ship climb, paraglider, and breakable field weapons as default open-map tools before treating ordered dungeon keys as the main progress gate (BOTW set aside linear dungeon-order conventions).
anti_lessons: Do not copy weapon durability without flooding the world with restock points and alternate tool types — durability alone without abundance only produces inventory anxiety.
Do not treat open world as content volume; without consistent state rules, the map is a checklist of one-off scripts.

system_bom.note: exploration, combat, and shrine solves share chemistry and physics; stamina gates climb/glide/swim/run while cooking funds hearts, stamina, and resistance for the next push
weapon and shield slots are temporary budgets that force field restock exploration
cooking converts field ingredients into stamina, hearts, and resistance consumables that fund the next climb or weather zone
shrines grant Spirit Orbs for heart/stamina vessels; towers reveal map sectors and enable fast travel — both gate options without linear story order

auto and manual saves preserve player progress across sessions and interruptions; frequent autosaves (including shrine approach and timed intervals) bank progress for multi-session return; the card does not claim durable material/chemistry object-state across reloads or enumerate specific inventory/map/shrine fields
death ends the attempt; Continue loads the prior save and refills Link to three hearts; uncommitted post-save work is lost beyond what that save holds

citations.claim: Moat and chemistry depth entry: chemistry is a rule-based state calculator (elements change materials and each other; materials do not change each other) paired with physics; element/object interaction enables unique player solutions to problems (including combat examples), not universal any-object rewrite of all domains.
Chemistry framed as state calculator alongside physics; almost every object and element is linked for unique puzzle/challenge solutions (GDC 2017 coverage).
Weapon durability budget: Fujibayashi and Aonuma describe breakable weapons as making swing decisions strategic (when and on whom to spend a weapon).
Inventory BOM: durability restock pressure drives exploration for replacement weapons.
Stamina vessel progression: Spirit Orbs from shrines trade for Stamina Vessels that expand the stamina wheel used for climbing and gliding.
Shared stamina pool: Link is limited by stamina while running, climbing, swimming, and gliding; Spirit Orbs expand health or stamina.
Feel signature experimental solutions emerge from interconnected physics and chemistry rules rather than scripted paths.

Second-session incentive structure: breakable weapons create exploration pressure for replacements; fire, ice, electricity interactions and material states are the reusable problem-solving vocabulary (not observed second-session return routing).
Cooking converts ingredients into food and elixirs that restore hearts/stamina and grant resistance buffs for weather and combat.
Crafting BOM: cooking is the consumable path that funds stamina and resistance for the next route.
Shrine function: completing Ancient Shrines awards Spirit Orbs spent at Goddess Statues for hearts or stamina.
Tower function: Sheikah Towers reveal map sectors and serve as vantage and fast-travel anchors once activated.
Quests BOM: shrine and tower systems gate map options and permanent stats without requiring linear story order.

Save-state BOM: auto-save and manual-save preserve progress across multi-session play and interruptions; autosaves fire on shrine approach, campfire, stable, chest, and timed intervals so progress can bank between sessions. Specific inventory/map/shrine field enumeration and chemistry/material world-state across reloads are not claimed.
citations.source: GameRant, 'Breath Of The Wild: How To Save' (manual save path; autosave on shrine approach, campfire, stable, chest, and timed intervals; little chance of losing significant progress): https://web.archive.org/web/20230528193236/https://gamerant.com/breath-of-the-wild-how-to-save/ ; Eiji Aonuma (Waypoint interview via Zelda Dungeon): auto-save and manual-save so battery/session interruption does not erase progress: https://www.zeldadungeon.net/breath-of-the-wild-has-two-saving-methods/

citations.claim: Win-loss-death BOM: choosing Continue after dying loads the save and gives Link three hearts; retained state is whatever that save holds, not post-save work.
citations.source: Zelda Wiki, Glitches in Breath of the Wild: 'Choosing to continue after dying will load the save but give Link 3 hearts': https://web.archive.org/web/20241227000208/https://zelda.fandom.com/wiki/Glitches_in_Breath_of_the_Wild ; GameRant, 'Breath Of The Wild: How To Save' (autosave on shrine approach and other progress moments; recoverable save slots): https://web.archive.org/web/20230528193236/https://gamerant.com/breath-of-the-wild-how-to-save/

citations.claim: Play-loop BOM chemistry/physics share: exploration and combat use the same chemistry and physics rules for player-authored solutions.
Packaging lesson 1 (labeled Inference): Great Plateau acts as a closed tutorial bed for climb, paraglider exit, Sheikah runes, and shrine rules before Hyrule scale.

Packaging lesson 2 (labeled Inference): climb, paraglider, and breakable weapons/shields are default open-map tools; production set aside ordered-dungeon-key conventions so dungeon order is not the main progress gate.
Anti-lesson durability without restock: designers and coverage treat abundant field weapons as the counterpart to breakage.
Anti-lesson open-world volume: GDC coverage stresses consistent chemistry/physics lies over one-off scripts.
Play-loop BOM stamina and cooking: stamina limits climb/glide/swim/run; cooking restores health/stamina and grants resistance buffs.
